title: Hydrogen Industry Urges Congress to Preserve Section 45V Tax Credit for Clean Energy Leadership
content: A coalition of businesses and organizations from the U.S. hydrogen industry is urging congressional leaders to maintain the Section 45V tax credit for clean hydrogen production. They argue that this incentive is crucial for securing U.S. energy dominance, boosting economic growth, and enhancing national security by reducing reliance on foreign energy. The industry, poised to invest billions following delayed regulatory guidance, warns that without the credit, jobs and investments could shift overseas. They call on lawmakers to preserve the tax incentive as they consider major tax legislation in 2025.
author: Aru Somya
